_templateName: Prevent duplicate webhook executions with AARI idempotency gate
_templateDescription:
Who this template is for

This template is for anyone running n8n workflows that receive webhooks and perform side effects such as payments, emails, database inserts, or API calls.

Webhook providers commonly use at-least-once delivery. If a webhook request times out or fails, the provider retries the same event. From n8n’s perspective this appears as a new trigger, which can cause the workflow to execute twice.

How it works

This workflow adds an idempotency check before any side effect runs.

When the first event arrives, the workflow records its idempotency key and proceeds normally. If the same event arrives again within 24 hours, the gate returns a BLOCK decision and the workflow stops before any side effects execute.

The webhook responds immediately with a 200 OK response., so providers do not retry due to workflow execution time. The idempotency gate then decides whether the workflow should proceed or stop.

This prevents duplicate executions such as:

duplicate payments
duplicate confirmation emails
duplicate database rows
duplicate external API calls

How to set it up

Setup usually takes about 2 minutes.

Create a free AARI API key at api.getaari.com/n8n
In n8n go to Credentials → New → Header Auth
Set the header name to X-API-Key
Paste your API key as the value
Select this credential in the AARI idempotency gate node
Replace the placeholder action node with your real workflow action (Stripe, Gmail, Postgres, etc.)

Detailed instructions are included in the sticky notes inside the workflow.

Requirements

n8n version 1.0 or later
AARI API key (free tier available)

Customization

You may need to adjust the idempotency_key depending on your webhook payload.

Examples:

Stripe webhook events: {{ $json.id }}
Shopify order events: {{ $json.order_id }}
Generic events: combine stable fields such as {{ $json.type }}:{{ $json.created }}
(for example event type + creation time)

The key should uniquely identify the event so duplicates can be detected reliably.
name: Prevent duplicate webhook executions in n8n

parameters.content:
## Prevent duplicate webhook executions

Many webhook providers use at-least-once delivery.
If a webhook times out or fails, the same event may be sent again.
From n8n's perspective it looks like a new trigger — the workflow executes twice.

This template adds an idempotency check before any side effect:
• Stripe charge
• Email send
• Database insert
• API call

If the same `idempotency_key` appears again within 24 hours,
AARI returns `decision: BLOCK` and the workflow stops.

────────────────────────────
Fingerprint fallback order
────────────────────────────
1. body.id / event_id / eventId / webhook_id
2. headers x-event-id / x-request-id
3. type:event:action:created:data.object.id:order_id:…
4. execution.id (same-execution only — not retry-safe)

────────────────────────────
Who this is for
────────────────────────────
Anyone running n8n workflows that receive webhooks and perform side effects — payments, emails, DB writes, API calls.

────────────────────────────
Requirements
────────────────────────────
Free AARI account → api.getaari.com/n8n
2,500 gate calls/month · No credit card

## Setup (2 minutes)

**1. Get a free AARI API key:**
https://api.getaari.com/n8n

**2. Create n8n credential:**
Credentials → New → Header Auth
Header name: `X-API-Key`
Value: your AARI key

Select it in the **AARI idempotency gate** node.

**3. idempotency_key is pre-set** — auto-detects the event ID from the payload (Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, etc.) or falls back to a body fingerprint.

**4. Connect your action:**
Replace **✅ Run your action here** with your real node — Stripe charge, send email, DB insert, API call, etc.

## Test it

Send the same webhook payload twice.

First call → `decision: ALLOW`
Second call → `decision: BLOCK`

The duplicate is caught before any action runs.

notes:
Prevent duplicate webhook executions using AARI idempotency.

Quick start:
1. Get a free key: https://api.getaari.com/n8n
2. Create Header Auth credential: X-API-Key → your key
3. Replace 'Run your action here' with your real action node
